Add comprehensive documentation suite (10 MD guides + HTML docs page)

Markdown docs (docs/):
- README.md: docs index with links to all guides
- FAQ.md: 30+ Q&As organized by category
- WIREGUARD.md: complete config parameter reference with examples
- OPENVPN.md: full server/client config reference with certificates
- STEP-BY-STEP.md: zero-to-VPN guide (7 parts)
- ARCHITECTURE.md: app internals, CSS/JS/i18n system
- FREEBOX.md: Freebox VPN client + server setup
- RASPBERRY-PI.md: full Pi deployment with security hardening
- DOCKER.md: containerized deployment guide
- LAUNCH-SCRIPT.md: script reference with all menu options

HTML docs (docs.html):
- Standalone documentation page with same theme system
- Sidebar navigation with search
- All content rendered as styled HTML sections
- Responsive (mobile hamburger menu)
- Print-friendly CSS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add variable binding, def, reduce, foreach, label-break (#22)

Major language features:
- $var variable binding with as-patterns (simple, array, object destructuring)
- def for user-defined functions with filter arguments
- reduce expr as $var (init; update)
- foreach expr as $var (init; update; extract)
- label $name | ... break $name
- {$var} shorthand in object construction

Interpreter refactored to carry Env (variable scope + function registry).

Official test suite: 292 → 344 passing (+52)
- variables: 0/16 → 14/16
- def: 4/20 → 15/20

Closes #20, closes #3, closes #4

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: NPC 국가/장수 금 증발 버그 수정 (legacy 이벤트 스케줄 준수)

Root cause: 수입/봉급/유지비 감소가 legacy 스케줄(월 1=금, 월 7=쌀)이 아닌
매 턴 실행되어 연간 12배 드레인 발생. NationAI의 bill 산식 오류와
GeneralAI의 무조건 헌납 분기가 드레인을 가속화.

## 엔진 수정
- EconomyService.preUpdateMonthly: processIncome/processWarIncome 호출 제거 (no-op)
  legacy func_gamerule.php:189는 income 처리 안 함
- EconomyService.postUpdateMonthly: processSemiAnnual 호출 제거
- EconomyService.processIncome/processSemiAnnual: resourceType 필수 인자,
  해당 자원만 income/봉급/decay 처리 (legacy ProcessIncome.php 분리 패턴)
- EconomyService.processIncomeEvent/processSemiAnnualEvent: resource 인자 추가
- EconomyService.processWarIncomeEvent: 테스트용 public entry point 추가
- ProcessIncomeAction/ProcessSemiAnnualAction: context.params["resource"] 읽어 전달
- NationAI.adjustTaxAndBill: 잘못된 (generals+cities)*bill 산식을
  legacy sum(getBill(dedication))로 교체, min bill 20
- GeneralAI.doDonate: reqGold*5 무조건 donate 분기에 확률 게이트 추가

## 드레인 수치 (수정 전 → 후)
- 국가 금: 봉급 12x/년 → 1x/년 (금), 1x/년 (쌀)
- 장수 금 유지비 감소: 11.5%/년 → 3%/년 (1만 초과 구간)
- 장수 헌납: 1만 초과 시 100% → 확률 게이트 적용

## 테스트 업데이트
- processMonthly/preUpdateMonthly 기반 테스트를 event-driven API로 마이그레이션
- NumericParityGoldenTest: 버그 기반 golden value를 범위 기반 sanity check로 교체
- EconomyIntegrationParityTest: runMonth(w) 헬퍼로 legacy-correct 스케줄 재현

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Shift primary voice from Taglish to conversational Filipino (#25)

Triggered by a user review flagging Taglish as demographically mismatched
for AKBai's MSME target (30-50 y/o sari-sari operators, home bakers,
provincial sellers). Linguistic research confirmed the distinction is
syntactic, not vocabulary: Taglish uses English SVO word order with
Filipino words sprinkled in; conversational Filipino uses a Filipino
syntactic frame (VSO, second-position enclitic pronouns, Filipino
conjunctions/prepositions/time adverbs) with English retained only for
technical terms.

The diagnostic marker: "bago i-save natin" is Taglish (enclitic buried
after verb); "bago natin i-save" is conversational Filipino (enclitic in
second position after the conjunction, per Wackernagel's Law).

The 8 Taglish markers now explicitly banned in Kai's output:
 1. Enclitic misplacement after conjunctions
 2. English conjunctions (if/before/because/when -> kung/bago/kasi/kapag)
 3. English prepositions (based sa -> ayon sa / batay sa)
 4. English time adverbs (this week -> ngayong linggo)
 5. Bare English verbs (save mo -> i-save mo)
 6. English comparatives (more -> mas)
 7. "yung" for definite objects in written output (yung resibo -> ang resibo)
 8. English SVO word order

English is retained only for BIR/tax terms, Filipinized verbs with
i-/mag-/na- affixes, brand names, numbers/currency/dates, and sparing
casual interjections.

Changes span 9 layers executed in parallel by specialized sub-agents:
 - Layer 1: Core definitions (CLAUDE.md Rule 5, master brief, brand-context,
   glossary, project-context)
 - Layer 2: Rewrote + renamed copy guides. taglish-copy-guide.md ->
   conversational-filipino-copy-guide.md (489 lines, new Section 13 Quick
   Reference Card). taglish-manual.md -> conversational-filipino-manual.md
   (234 lines).
 - Layer 3: Extended filipino-text-vernacular.md with new sections on what
   Kai should NOT use, voice toolkit summary, and regional awareness.
 - Layers 4-6: Terminology sweep across 40+ skill files, agent definitions,
   reference docs, and gap registry (G2/G3/G8 renamed).
 - Layer 7: Rewrote core-persona.ts voice rules with full Filipino
   syntactic frame. Synced features.ts feature prompts. Bumped
   prompt-library.md to v1.1.0 and synced Section 1 body to match live
   prompt. Added 9 new regression tests in prompt-regression.test.ts
   Group 6, one per marker.
 - Layer 8a: 10 landing-page fixes including the critical "100% Taglish"
   -> "100% Filipino" badge, removal of the "Your AI Business Partner"
   English supertitle, and rewriting of the Meet Kai body and Step 2
   body to use Filipino time adverbs.
 - Layer 8b: ~60 edits across brand/AKBai Brand Book.html, Archive
   competitive brief, ops playbook, vision doc, project plugin strategy,
   skills utilization guide, and 5 marketing markdowns.
 - Layer 9: 9 user-visible frontend component fixes (step-bir-consent,
   errors.ts, chat-interface, first-responses, notifications, welcome-tour,
   expenses-summary) + 26 test description renames across 20 test files.

A team-lead cross-layer consistency audit caught a critical drift: the
prompt-library.md Section 1 header was bumped to v1.1.0 but the prompt
code block was still the v1.0.0 Taglish version, including a "Based sa
trend..." canonical example that directly violated the new "based sa"
ban. Fixed by syncing the body to match core-persona.ts verbatim. The
audit also flagged 15 command-skill files (akbai-delivery/skills/*/commands/*
subfolder) that had been missed by the initial sweep — all fixed in the
final pass.

Enclitic rule refinement (native-speaker correction): the strict
second-position enclitic rule applies cleanly only after bago, kapag/pag,
and habang. After "para + verb" native speakers place the enclitic after
the verb ("para ma-check mo"), and with "kung gusto + verb" the natural
form is "kung gusto mong i-edit". Copy guide Rule 2 documents these
exceptions explicitly.

Tests: 770/770 passing across 52 test files. Zero runtime regressions.
The new Group 6 regression tests assert at the prompt-construction level
(deterministic CI, no API calls). A behavioral smoke test against the
live chat endpoint is recommended as a pre-ship validation.

Deferred (non-blocking):
 - PDF regeneration: brand/AKBai Brand Book.pdf, Archive competitive
   brief PDF, and ops playbook PDF still reference old Taglish framing.
   HTML sources updated; PDFs need re-export.
 - project/*.pdf files without editable docx/html sources left untouched
   pending source location.
 - features.ts reply_drafter customer-matching rule deliberately keeps
   "Taglish" as a descriptive category for user input while enforcing
   "conversational Filipino" as Kai's output. Intentional asymmetry.

Files modified: 107 (including 2 file renames)
Reference documents updated: sprint-history.md (Sprint 11 retro entry),
project-context.md (Current Phase updated to Sprint 11 complete).

commit.message:
release: update to v1.0.0-RC3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Kill shelves, kill social links, full-width editorial layouts everywhere

The user flagged three things at once. Handled them in one coherent
pass since they all touch the same surfaces.

== Kill shelves ==

The wood / marble / glass / minimal "shelf" metaphor wasn't paying
off. The chrome fought the editorial dark layout instead of
complementing it, lists looked identical to bare lists no matter
which style was picked, and the metaphor got more confusing as
more primitives (lists, charts, lyric pins) appeared on the same
profile. Killed entirely:

  * Deleted src/lib/shelfStyles.ts
  * Deleted src/components/list/ListShelf.tsx
  * RecordShelf rewritten as a clean responsive cover grid (3-7
    columns depending on viewport) with title/artist/stars
    visible by default instead of hover-only — that was a real
    discoverability bug. Same filter/sort controls preserved.
  * ProfilePage no longer passes shelfStyle to RecordShelf
  * List page no longer queries profiles.shelf_style and renders
    items as a clean numbered tracklist (divide-y rows, big
    serif numerals, larger covers, generous py-6/py-7 row
    spacing) — looks magazine-grade without needing chrome
  * Settings: Appearance tab + the entire shelf-style picker
    deleted along with saveAppearance(), shelfStyle state, etc.

The shelf_style column stays in the database (no migration to
drop it — migrations are append-only and the column is harmless
when unread).

== Kill social links ==

Instagram / X / Spotify username inputs in Settings → Profile and
the corresponding render block on ProfilePage both removed. The
social_links column stays in the database for the same reason.
The whole feature was a half-built afterthought; the constitution
says Euterpy is the destination, not a hub for other platforms,
and these inputs were diluting that.

== Full-width editorial layouts ==

Every "default" page was a narrow centered box on desktop (max-w-sm
to max-w-4xl) — they read as cramped, generic dashboards instead
of editorial magazine spreads. Rebuilt every one as a two-column
layout that fills the editorial canvas:

  * /settings — was max-w-4xl single-column. Now max-w-7xl with
    a sticky lg:left sidebar nav (Profile / Privacy / Requests
    with descriptions under each label) and a right column for
    the active tab content. The Account section (Log out + Delete
    account) lives at the bottom of the right column, always
    visible regardless of which tab.

  * /welcome — both the question screen AND the finish screen
    were max-w-2xl centered. Now max-w-7xl two-column: editorial
    prompt on the left (sticky on desktop), search/results or
    confirmed pick on the right. Footer nav (← Previous / Skip
    for now) lives under the left column on desktop, under the
    page on mobile.

  * /login — was max-w-sm centered. Now max-w-7xl two-column:
    editorial welcome hero on the left ("The room is still
    here."), the form column on the right (still max-w-md
    because forms shouldn't stretch wide). Top brand bar +
    accent glow.

  * /signup — was max-w-sm centered with all 4 steps inside one
    container. Now max-w-7xl two-column: editorial brand hero
    on the left ("A music identity, handmade.") that stays
    sticky and consistent across all 4 steps, the form column
    on the right with the progress strip and step contents.
    The hero + the constant brand text on the left makes the
    multi-step flow feel like a magazine spread, not a wizard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(admin/leads): step 2 — Postgres-backed leads bandeja with custom statuses

Bloque 2, paso 2 del plan: backoffice de leads completo.

Schema:
- New tables: lead_statuses (configurable), leads, lead_notes
- Default statuses seeded only on first init (nuevo, contactado,
  cualificado, presupuestado, cerrado, perdido, descartado)
- Indexes on created_at, status_id, score_quality
- 'nuevo' marked is_default and protected from deletion

Repository (lib/db/repositories/leads.ts):
- saveLead — persists to PG, no-op if DB not configured
- listLeads with composite filters (status, quality, sinceDays, search)
- getLead, updateLeadStatus, listNotes, addNote
- listStatuses, upsertStatus, deleteStatus

Pipeline:
- DefaultLeadRepository.save now writes to PG AND best-effort to Sheets;
  PG failure throws, Sheets failure is silent. Email notify unchanged.

Endpoints:
- GET    /api/admin/leads             list + filters + statuses bundle
- GET    /api/admin/leads/[id]        full lead + notes
- PATCH  /api/admin/leads/[id]        change status
- POST   /api/admin/leads/[id]/notes  append note
- GET    /api/admin/leads/statuses    list
- POST   /api/admin/leads/statuses    upsert
- DELETE /api/admin/leads/statuses?id  remove (blocked for is_default)

UI:
- /admin/leads — bandeja con búsqueda, filtros (estado/calidad/fecha),
  tabla compacta con badges de score y status, click en fila abre
  drawer
- Drawer lateral 2xl: contacto + acciones (tel, email, WA prefilled),
  resumen del cliente destacado, datos rápidos, diagnóstico completo,
  UTMs, cambio de estado en vivo, notas internas en timeline
- /admin/leads/estados — CRUD de estados (color picker, label editable,
  posición numérica, borrado bloqueado para is_default)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Project-level layer detection: suppress duplicate Build steps

In a monolith repo, backend/frontend/agents exist ONCE for the whole
project. Previously every process without matched files showed "Build
Backend Services" even though the repo already has one.

Now the execution engine checks project-level layers from the full
repo file tree. If the PROJECT has a backend, individual processes
skip "Build Backend" and go straight to "Implement Requirements."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make message sending non-blocking by firing emails in background

The email notification (profile lookup + Resend API call) was running
synchronously, blocking the server action response for 1-2 seconds.
Now the message insert returns immediately and the email fires in a
detached async IIFE. Emails still send, they just don't hold up the UI.

commit.message:
feat(wizard): step 1 — early budget + urgency qualification + lead scoring

Captación / cualificación temprana (Bloque 1, paso 1 del plan):

Wizard:
- Reorder steps so cualificación happens first:
  tipo → presupuesto → urgencia → metros → barrio → … → contacto
- StepPresupuesto rewritten as range buttons (<40k / 40-80k / 80-150k /
  >150k / no-sé) instead of free numeric input. Easier, no commitment,
  better data for filtering.
- New StepUrgencia: este-mes / 1-3 meses / 3-6 meses / sin-fecha.
- Total steps: 10 → 11.

Tipos:
- WizardAnswers gains presupuestoRango + urgencia
- New types PresupuestoRango, Urgencia in lib/pricing/types

Cualificación:
- New lib/pricing/viability-budget.ts with:
  · isBudgetUnrealistic(): true if user range max < 50% of realistic min
  · scoreLead(): 0-100 score combining urgency + budget match + tipo,
    bucketed alto/medio/bajo

refactor: unified entity+building processing loop (C++ Logic.AI parity)

Replace 4-pass entity/building processing with single unified loop
matching C++ logic.cpp:284-339 exactly. Uses a single logicIdx counter
across all phases for correct source tag numbering.

Order: entities → buildings (with HPAD helicopter interleaving) →
reinforcements → aircraft. All inside _runCombat for building Firing_AI.

Confirms remaining ±1-5 divergence is NOT from processing order —
it's from behavioral differences in guard scan/combat outcomes.

54,938 tests pass, 12/12 scenarios pass parity.

v2.2: Display VPP event times in BST (Europe/London) not UTC

plugin.py:
- Add _local_time(dt, fmt) helper: converts UTC-aware datetime to
  Europe/London local time via pytz (already a dependency); falls back
  to UTC strftime if pytz unavailable
- Apply _local_time() to all VPP event display points:
    - "Event announced" log message
    - VPP device state (eventStartTime / eventEndTime)
    - Menu VPP status log
- Append " BST" label to announced/menu log lines for clarity

axle_api.py:
- Convert debug log times to Europe/London before formatting

Root cause: Axle API returns UTC ISO-8601 timestamps; strftime() on a
UTC datetime shows UTC, which is 1h behind BST (late Mar - late Oct).
The user's event appeared as 07:30-08:30 but the Axle email confirmed
08:30-09:30 BST — exactly 1 hour off.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): resolve pip-audit editable install error

--skip-editable flag does not reliably suppress the editable package
lookup. Instead, freeze exact installed versions and strip our own
package from the list before auditing.

  pip freeze | grep -v "^agent-container\|^-e " > /tmp/frozen-deps.txt
  pip-audit -r /tmp/frozen-deps.txt

This audits exact pinned third-party versions in the CI environment
without attempting to look up our own package on PyPI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
List pages now sit on the user's chosen shelf + Log out / Delete account

Three connected gaps the user flagged:

1. LISTS LOOK LIKE BARE LISTS, NOT SHELVES
   The shelf-style picker (wood / marble / glass / minimal) in
   Settings → Appearance has been sitting in the database driving
   the profile collection only. Lists have always rendered as a
   generic ordered <ol>. They should look like another shelf in
   the user's record store — same wood grain, same marble veining,
   same glass frosting.

   * Extracted SHELF_STYLES into /lib/shelfStyles.ts as a shared
     module so both the profile collection and the list page pull
     from one source of truth. Includes a resolveShelfStyle()
     helper for safely defaulting null/unknown values to minimal.
   * Refactored RecordShelf to import from the shared module
     instead of inlining ~150 lines of style config.
   * New ListShelf component — vertical numbered rows (cover +
     title + artist + caption) wrapped in the same frame + ledge +
     floor-shadow chrome RecordShelf uses. Minimal style preserves
     the original look (just a hairline ledge); the wood / marble /
     glass styles wrap items in their full back-wall + side-wall
     container so the wood grain etc is actually visible around
     the items.
   * /list/[id] page now SELECTs profiles.shelf_style alongside
     the rest of the author's profile fields and passes it to
     ListShelf. Replaced the inline <ol> block with <ListShelf
     items={items} shelfStyle={author.shelf_style} />.

2. NO EXPLICIT LOG OUT
   Sign Out existed but was buried at the bottom of the Profile
   tab where most users wouldn't look. Promoted to a dedicated
   Account section that lives below the tabs and is always
   visible regardless of which tab the user is on. Renamed to
   "Log out" since that's the noun users actually search for.

3. NO DELETE ACCOUNT
   Doesn't exist anywhere in the app. Now added end-to-end:

   * New POST /api/account/delete route. Authenticates via cookie
     session (never trusts a userId from the body), then uses the
     service-role client's auth.admin.deleteUser() to remove the
     auth.users row. The cascade chain handles everything else:
     auth.users → profiles → ratings / stories / lyric pins /
     lists / charts / follows / marks / echoes / letters / GTKM
     all go away automatically because every dependent table has
     ON DELETE CASCADE on its profiles FK (verified in migration
     001). Server signs the user out before responding so the
     client can't keep a stale refresh token.

   * Settings UI: Delete Account button in the new Account section,
     same height/weight as Log out but with red border accents to
     read as destructive. Click opens a typed-confirmation dialog
     ("type DELETE to confirm") with a magazine-style header
     ("Permanent action / Delete your account?"), an italic
     editorial blurb explaining what will be removed, the input
     field, and Cancel + Delete-forever buttons. The Delete button
     is disabled until the typed phrase exactly matches DELETE.
     Backdrop click closes the dialog (unless mid-deletion).

   The whole flow is gated so no accidental click can ever
   trigger an account deletion — the user has to type the word,
   read the warning, and press the destructive button.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix DLS EG envelope time generators using absolute timecent values

DLS articulator connections specify envelope times as absolute timecent
values, but MergeArticulatorGenerator was adding them to the SF2
defaults (e.g. AttackVolEnv=-12000, DecayVolEnv=-12000), causing the
effective times to be far shorter than intended.

For example, a DLS decay of 1952 timecents (~3 seconds) was being added
to the SF2 default -12000, yielding -10048 (~3ms) instead. With a zero
sustain level this made percussive sounds decay to silence almost
instantly rather than over the intended 3 seconds.

Change the merge strategy for the five volume envelope time generators
(Delay/Attack/Hold/Decay/Release) to SET rather than ADD, consistent
with the existing treatment of OverridingRootKey/Keynum/Velocity.
Generators absent from the DLS articulators (sentinel 0x7fffffff) are
left at their SF2 defaults as before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump to 10 pixels per tomato for faster mountain fill

1 pixel per tomato was too slow — 10 users would take 50 days.
Now each completed session earns 10 permanent pixels:

  1,980 grid cells / 10 pixels per tomato = 198 tomatoes needed
  10 users × 4 sessions/day = 40 tomatoes/day → ~5 days
  100 users = 400/day → ~12 hours
  Solo = 4/day → ~50 days

Local session: 10 staggered flingContributionPixel arcs (150ms apart)
Remote session: addRemoteContributionBurst(nick, 10)
Demo completion: same

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(core): propagate interceptor-modified command through chain

The handler invoker lambda was capturing the original command from the
outer scope instead of using the cmd parameter passed through the
interceptor chain, causing modifications made by interceptors to be
silently discarded.

Discovered via unit test in SimpleCommandBusTest.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

v2.1: Poll every 60s during cooling off; protect EMS mode from verify() during VPP

_vpp_poll_interval():
- Add VPP_COOLING_OFF to the 60s poll case (alongside VPP_ACTIVE)
- Previously fell through to 10-min normal interval, meaning we could take
  up to 10 min to detect Axle releasing the inverter; Axle typically releases
  within 1-2 min of an event ending, so 60s polling is essential

_verify_ems_registers():
- Skip EMS mode read/correct during VPP_ACTIVE and VPP_COOLING_OFF
- During these states Axle has (or is releasing) control of the inverter;
  writing to register 40031 could fight Axle's firmware or disrupt the
  handback sequence
- _vpp_check_axle_release() is solely responsible for reinstating Remote EMS
  once emsWorkMode (register 30003) returns to "Max Self Consumption"

Implements gcp/gcp_create_infra.py mirroring the AWS and Azure CLIs, with
all 9 commands: create, destroy, set-admin-password, upgrade-content,
upgrade-panos, upgrade-antivirus, create-image, create-custom-image, and
create-custom-image-restart.

GCP-specific adaptations:
- 3 separate VPC networks (mgmt/untrust/trust) per GCP's multi-NIC requirement
- Bootstrap via instance metadata key-value pairs instead of user-data/custom_data
- Image creation via compute_v1 images.insert(source_disk=...) — no generalize step
- Ordered destroy (no Resource Group cascade): instance → IPs → firewall rules → subnets → networks
- Async operation polling via zone/region/global ops clients

Also adds gcp_marketplace_explorer.py, marketplace_images.yaml, requirements.txt,
README.md, and .gitignore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: server merge overwrites user status edits on sync

Merge logic was applying status preservation for ALL updates, including
explicit user edits from saveLeadField. Now preservation only kicks in
when the incoming status is NEU (= generation run). User edits that send
a real status (VERKAUFSGESPRAECH etc.) are always accepted by the server.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(encoding): stop HTML-encoding plain text fields, add DB migration script

Review text, comments, usernames, and forum posts were stored with HTML
entities (' for apostrophes, & etc.) because sanitizeHTML() was
applied before writing to MongoDB. Since all these fields are rendered as
React text nodes (never dangerouslySetInnerHTML), React displayed the
literal entity strings instead of the intended characters.

- Add sanitizePlainText() to validation.ts — strips control chars only,
  no HTML encoding (React JSX handles XSS for text nodes)
- Switch all plain-text API routes to use sanitizePlainText instead of
  sanitizeHTML (reviews, comments, user name, forum threads/comments)
- Add scripts/decode-review-text.ts — one-time migration to decode
  existing stored entities; supports dry-run (default) and --apply mode

commit.message:
feat: provider hardening — MessageConverter, StubProvider, Vertex tool fix (TRP-GOL-9)

Three enforcement layers to prevent TRP-BUG-2 (silently dropped messages):

1. COMPILE-TIME: MessageConverter interface with per-role methods.
   var _ MessageConverter = (*VertexConverter)(nil) — missing role = won't build.

2. UNIT: 8 pure function tests for convertAllMessages() covering all roles:
   user, assistant, assistant+tool_use, system, tool, empty, unknown, multi-system.

3. TESTKIT: StubProvider with TextResponse/ToolCallResponse/ToolCall helpers,
   call recording, error mode, concurrent safety. 8 tests.

Vertex fix: convertMessages() now handles system (extracted to param),
tool (NewUserMessage with tool_result block), and assistant with ToolCalls
(preserves tool_use content blocks). System prompt passed via req.System.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

One tomato = one permanent pixel + friendlier status labels

Two changes per user request:

1. Contribution pixel economy reworked — "토마토 하나당 한픽셀":
   - During focus: Steve's digs fling TEMPORARY particles that fade
     on landing (new flingTemporaryParticle). No grid cells consumed.
   - On session completion: ONE permanent pixel arcs from Steve to
     the mountain grid (the reward moment for finishing a tomato).
   - Remote session completions: ONE permanent pixel per session.
   - Demo ambient bursts: now fling temporary particles only; demo
     session completions still earn one real pixel each.
   - Grid cells: 1,980 (unchanged). Expected fill times:
       100 users × 4 sessions/day → ~5 days
       10 users  × 4 sessions/day → ~50 days
       Solo      × 4 sessions/day → ~495 days (long but collective)

2. Status pill text friendlier:
   - "🟢 ~128 FOCUSING" → "📖 128 STUDYING TOGETHER"
   - "🟢 ALONE · BE FIRST" → "📖 STUDYING ALONE"
   - Users immediately understand "wow, 128 people are studying
     with me right now" instead of the abstract "FOCUSING" label.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v2.0: Night export respects upcoming VPP event energy reserve

battery_manager.py:
- Add vpp_reserved_kwh field to ManagerSnapshot (defaults 0.0 — no impact
  when no VPP event pending)
- _check_night_export(): subtract vpp_reserved_kwh from exportable surplus
  so overnight export stops before draining the energy needed for a morning
  VPP dispatch
- Include VPP reserve in stop/start decision reason strings for visibility

plugin.py:
- Pre-compute _vpp_reserved_kwh (max_export_kw × duration_hrs /
  VPP_DISCHARGE_EFFICIENCY) when state is ANNOUNCED or PRE_CHARGING
- Pass to ManagerSnapshot so battery_manager stays stateless

Effect: if a 1-hour VPP event at 4 kW is announced (= 4.12 kWh reserve),
night export treats the battery floor as dawn_target + 1.0 kWh buffer +
4.12 kWh VPP reserve. Export halts before that energy is consumed, ensuring
the battery still holds enough to service the event without any grid import.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
style(sobre-cota): add subtle technical grid + warm glows background

Adds a fixed decorative layer behind the page: a fine 64px gold grid
masked by a radial fade, a coarser 256px cream grid for depth, two
soft blurred glows (gold top-right, cream bottom-left), and a single
vertical measure line. All sections lifted to z-1 so the decoration
sits behind. Pure CSS, no images.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: barrel sentient investigation + HPAD helicopter guard AI

Investigation confirms ALL buildings (including BARL/BRL3 barrels)
are sentient in C++ and consume RNG. The ±2 divergence on SCG03EA/
SCG13EA is from architectural entity/building processing order
(C++ single Logic loop vs TS multi-pass), not missing buildings.

New tests: barrel sentient parity (4 tests), helicopter guard AI (14).
Total: 54,938 tests, 925 files, 0 failures.
